rationale: Both evaluations award an identical 6/10 and rest on the same substantive concern — no direct Java/Kotlin or Spring/enterprise-framework experience, with primary experience in JavaScript/TypeScript. The wording differs slightly ('software craftsmanship and testing' vs 'full-stack development', 'non-Java ecosystems' vs 'non-JVM languages'), but these are reworded paraphrases of the same job-relevant judgments and fall within ordinary run-to-run noise. Nothing in the variant keys off the name change.
